macro_segments.title: Rava's Ruling on Selling a Synagogue: The Role of Community Leadership and Kedusha Transfer

micro_segments.title: Rava's statement that restrictions on use apply only when not sold by seven elders in presence of city
Explanation of shiva tuvei ha'ir as lay leadership and discussion of exemption when elders sell with community present
Even drinking beer in the synagogue becomes permitted when properly sold by elders with community
Debate over whether lemishta bei shichra means drinking with proceeds or in the synagogue itself
Ramban and other Rishonim's approaches to understanding kedusha transfer and use of money

macro_segments.title: Case of Ravina: Purchasing Fallen Synagogue Materials for New Construction

micro_segments.title: Ravina seeks permission from Rava to plant on fallen synagogue, told to purchase from elders with community present

macro_segments.title: Rami bar Abba's Dilemma: Demolishing Old Synagogue to Build New One with Salvaged Materials

micro_segments.title: Rami bar Abba wants to knock down old unused synagogue to use beams and stones for new one
Rav Chisda's rule from Bava Batra: don't demolish one synagogue until building another
Rationale of peshiuta (negligence): fear people won't complete the new synagogue
Rami's case differs since he's actively building another, yet Rav Pappa and Rav Huna forbid it

macro_segments.title: The Role of Sale and Exchange in Desacralifying Synagogue Status

micro_segments.title: Rava: exchange and sale permit use of synagogue; kedusha disappears through these transactions
Renting or collateral forbidden without sale; kedusha retained until sold
Bricks set aside for synagogue: sanctified only when built-in, not merely accumulated
Hazmanah (designation) insufficient for bricks not yet built; weaving vs. spun thread analogy
Debate over gift as mechanism to remove kedusha: exchange model vs. benefit exchange

macro_segments.title: Tashmishei Mitzvah vs. Tashmishei Kedusha: Distinguishing Object Status and Sanctity

micro_segments.title: Braisa distinguishes tashmishei mitzvah (discardable) from tashmishei kedusha (must be buried)
Examples of tashmishei mitzvah: sukkah, lulav, shofar, tzitzit
Examples of tashmishei kedusha: covers for sefarim, tefillin, mezuzot and their straps
Rava's development: bimah (pulpit) reassessed from tashmish de'tashmish to tashmish kedusha
Parochet (curtain) similarly upgraded to tashmish kedusha due to occasional direct contact

macro_segments.title: Case of Roman Jewish Synagogue: Ritual Purity Barriers and Ark Placement

micro_segments.title: Roman Jewish synagogue in Babylonia with opening to room containing corpse; priests want to pray
Rava suggests placing wooden ark in doorway as barrier since kli etz made for rest cannot become tamei
Rabbis object: ark sometimes moved even when containing sefer torah, so not made for staying in place
Rava concedes; situation remains problematic without solution

macro_segments.title: Tashmishei Mitzvah vs. Tashmishei Kedusha: Distinguishing Object Status and Sanctity (continued)

micro_segments.title: Worn covers for chumashim: can become shrouds for burial (Mar Zutra's ruling)
Rav Acha: bury worn sefer torah near talmid chacham in pottery vessel for preservation

macro_segments.title: Hierarchy of Kedusha: Synagogue vs. Study Hall (Beit Midrash)

micro_segments.title: Rav Pappa quoting Rava: converting study hall to synagogue forbidden; synagogue to study hall permitted
Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi permits converting synagogue to study hall, implying study hall has higher status
Debate over definition of bayit gadol: place where Torah grows vs. where prayer grows
Verses support both views: yagdil Torah (Torah) vs. great deeds of Elisha (prayer)
Conclusion: Rabbi Yehoshua ben Levi favors Torah study, but rabbis still debate primacy

macro_segments.title: Using Proceeds of Sefer Torah Sale: Same Level vs. Upgrade Requirement

micro_segments.title: Question: can proceeds from selling old sefer torah be used to buy new one (same level)?
Debate: if must go up, but no level higher than sefer torah, is staying at same level permitted?
Mishnah rule tested: selling Torah forbids buying other sefarim; unclear on same-level use
Wrapping analogy: sefer torah wraps in chumash wraps permitted; reverse forbidden; same level unclear
Gemara concludes cannot determine from sources whether same-level use of sefer torah proceeds is allowed

topical_tags.term: Shiva Tuvei Ha'ir (Seven Good People of City)
Mamad Anshei Ha'ir (Presence of City People)
